introSection: Steel Force is a Morgan hyper roller coaster located at Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The former Bolliger & Mabillard stand-up roller coaster at Cedar Point, Mantis, was originally going to be named "Banshee". However, the name was changed due to sensitivity reasons (Banshee is an Irish myth, supposedly seeing or hearing one means death). The logo was then adopted by Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom, another Cedar Fair-owned park to use for Steel Force. The name Banshee would later be used for the 2014 inverted coaster Banshee at Kings Island.

On September 16, 1996, Dorney Park announced that Steel Force would be coming to the park.

Steel Force opened on May 30, 1997 following a preview event the day before.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Steel Force was designed by D.H. Morgan Manufacturing, who manufactured the trains and track. The support structure was fabricated by Davis Construction, based in Emmaus, Pennsylvania.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: Steel Force has an out and back layout. The chain lift hill leads straight into the tall first drop, at the bottom of which is a  long tunnel. A tall hill follows. Another hill follows which leads into the 510° helix as the track curves to the right. Now at ground level, the train twists to the left and rises into the mid-course brake run. Now heading back towards the station, the train dives and another hill follows, which leads into another long tunnel. Another three hills follow before the final brake run.

fullText:
Steel Force is a Morgan hyper roller coaster located at Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA.

The former Bolliger & Mabillard stand-up roller coaster at Cedar Point, Mantis, was originally going to be named "Banshee". However, the name was changed due to sensitivity reasons (Banshee is an Irish myth, supposedly seeing or hearing one means death). The logo was then adopted by Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom, another Cedar Fair-owned park to use for Steel Force. The name Banshee would later be used for the 2014 inverted coaster Banshee at Kings Island.

On September 16, 1996, Dorney Park announced that Steel Force would be coming to the park.

Steel Force opened on May 30, 1997 following a preview event the day before.

Steel Force was designed by D.H. Morgan Manufacturing, who manufactured the trains and track. The support structure was fabricated by Davis Construction, based in Emmaus, Pennsylvania.

Steel Force has an out and back layout. The chain lift hill leads straight into the tall first drop, at the bottom of which is a  long tunnel. A tall hill follows. Another hill follows which leads into the 510° helix as the track curves to the right. Now at ground level, the train twists to the left and rises into the mid-course brake run. Now heading back towards the station, the train dives and another hill follows, which leads into another long tunnel. Another three hills follow before the final brake run.
